title: Celastrus euphlebiphyllus
intro: Celastrus euphlebiphyllus, also known as the Himalayan staff vine or staff tree, is a climbing plant native to the Himalayas, belonging to the Celastraceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Celastrus euphlebiphyllus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Celastrus euphlebiphyllus is commonly used to prepare herbal decoctions and infusions, as well as being incorporated into herbal teas and tinctures.
taxonomy: Celastrus euphlebiphyllus is classified within the plant family Celastraceae. This family, also known as the staff vine family, comprises approximately 70 genera and 1000 species. Celastrus euphlebiphyllus is a species of evergreen climbing vine native to Asia. The species is further classified within the genus Celastrus, which contains approximately 60 species. The classification of Celastrus euphlebiphyllus reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Celastraceae family.

Its extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is also reported to have anti-cancer properties, with some studies indicating its ability to inhibit the growth of cancer cells.
